summary: Complete testing of AddDocumentModal feature. The modal opens correctly on both Dashboard and CaseDetail pages (NOT redirecting to /upload). All modal elements verified: upload drop zone, context textarea (0/500 counter), 'Analyze with Archer' button (disabled until file selected), close button. Backend POST /api/documents/upload accepts case_id and user_context Form fields correctly.

1_dashboard_add_doc_button.evidence: Found 'Add document' button with data-testid='add-doc-btn' on Dashboard page

2_dashboard_modal_opens_not_redirect.evidence: Clicking 'Add document' on Dashboard opens modal (NOT redirect to /upload). URL stays at /dashboard

4_upload_drop_zone_visible.evidence: Upload drop zone with data-testid='upload-drop-zone' is visible. Shows 'Drag & drop your file here' and 'or click to browse'

5_context_textarea_visible.evidence: Context textarea with data-testid='doc-context-input' is visible with placeholder text

6_analyze_button_disabled_no_file.evidence: 'Analyze with Archer' button (data-testid='analyze-with-archer-btn') is disabled when no file selected

7_close_button_visible.evidence: Close button with data-testid='close-add-doc-modal' is visible

8_character_counter_visible.evidence: Character counter shows '0/500 characters' below context textarea

9_modal_closes_on_x_click.evidence: Clicking X button closes modal, stays on same page

10_casedetail_add_doc_button.evidence: Found 'Add document' button with data-testid='add-doc-btn' on CaseDetail page (/cases/{case_id})

11_casedetail_modal_opens_not_redirect.evidence: Clicking 'Add document' on CaseDetail opens modal (NOT redirect to /upload). URL stays at /cases/{case_id}

12_casedetail_modal_all_elements.evidence: CaseDetail modal has all elements: upload-drop-zone, doc-context-input, analyze-with-archer-btn, close-add-doc-modal

backend_tests.test_upload_endpoint_accepts_case_id_for: PASS - POST /api/documents/upload accepts case_id as Form field and links document to existing case
backend_tests.test_upload_endpoint_accepts_user_contex: PASS - POST /api/documents/upload accepts user_context as Form field (500 chars max)
backend_tests.test_upload_without_case_id_creates_new_: PASS - Uploading without case_id creates a new case

critical_code_review_comments: AddDocumentModal.js is well-structured with proper data-testid attributes for all interactive elements
Modal correctly uses axios.post with multipart/form-data and withCredentials: true
Dashboard.js integrates modal with showAddDoc state and onUploadComplete callback that calls fetchCases()
CaseDetail.js integrates modal with showAddDoc state and onUploadComplete callback that calls fetchData()
Backend POST /api/documents/upload at line 2953 correctly accepts case_id and user_context as Form fields
When case_id is provided, document is linked to existing case; when not provided, new case is created
Modal has 3 language variants (en, fr, nl) matching user profile language
seed_data_creation: None - used existing seeded data with 28 cases
context_for_next_testing_agent: AddDocumentModal feature is fully implemented and tested. The modal opens on both Dashboard and CaseDetail pages when clicking 'Add document' button. It does NOT redirect to /upload. The modal has: drag & drop upload zone, context textarea (500 chars max), 'Analyze with Archer' button (disabled until file selected), Cancel button, and X close button. Backend POST /api/documents/upload accepts case_id and user_context Form fields. After upload, the modal closes and fetchCases/fetchData is called to refresh case data.
screenshots: .screenshots/add_doc_modal_dashboard.png - AddDocumentModal on Dashboard page with all elements visible
.screenshots/add_doc_modal_casedetail.png - AddDocumentModal on CaseDetail page with all elements visible
